lesson.menu_desc: Write a clear email after a meeting -- subject, recap, and next steps -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 16 -- The Follow-Up Email -- Writing After a Meeting
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Percival JR | Aula 16 -- The Follow-Up Email

listenings.text: Hi Percival, this is Karen. Great meeting today, thank you. Could you send a short follow-up email to the team this afternoon? Please keep it simple. Start with a clear subject line, something like Risk Review, Next Steps. Then a short greeting, and three lines: thank everyone, write a quick recap of what we decided, and list the two action items with their owners. Please attach the supplier report, and add Daniel as a recipient in copy. End with a polite closing, Best regards, and your name. If you have time, send me the draft first, and I will reply with any small changes before you send it. Thank you so much.

Okay, let me read my draft out loud before I send it. Subject: Risk Review, Next Steps. Greeting: Dear team. First line: Thank you all for your time in today's meeting. Second line: As a quick recap, we agreed to update the supplier list and to test the new control by Friday. Third line: Please find attached the supplier report for your reference. I would be grateful if you could send your comments by Thursday. Closing: Best regards, Percival. I will add Karen and Daniel as recipients, put Daniel in copy, attach the report, and then send it. I think this draft is clear and short. I will forward it to my manager too.

extra_audio.text: First, Percival writes a clear subject line for the email. Then, he adds a short greeting and thanks the team. After that, he writes a quick recap of what the meeting decided. Later, he lists the two action items and attaches the supplier report. Finally, he adds a polite closing, checks the draft, and sends the email.
